positive_feedback: AskUserQuestion is now an explicit least-privilege capability alongside Read and Write, matching the Purpose contract, goal-seek checklist, and R1 prompt rather than relying on an undeclared interactive tool.

IN1 and IN2 retain a narrow, auditable artifact boundary: options.json has selected_integrations/rejected arrays, selected entries carry id/name/tier, rejected entries require a non-empty reason, and selected IDs are constrained to the read-only shared integration catalog.

OUT1 remains a design claim: catalog lookup and options.json generation are the only responsibilities, while integration execution, authentication, publication, and catalog extension stay delegated to other phases.

OUT2 now records the behavioral consequence of adding AskUserQuestion as a separate live-trial criterion: candidates must be presented one question at a time, real selected/rejected answers and rejection reasons must be captured, and the result must be reflected in options.json. This preserves the protocol boundary between static design adequacy and Gate D execution evidence.

The R1 prompt bounds the interactive loop to five questions, prohibits parallel questions, and hands only options.json plus the exit status back to the parent, preventing unbounded dialogue and orchestration leakage.

notes: Genuine two-pass review. Pass 1 found that adding AskUserQuestion raised the skill to live acceptance tier while feedback_contract had no live-trial criterion; lint-feedback-contract failed specifically for run-intake-option-catalog. The minimal correction added purpose-specific OUT2 without replacing the static OUT1 design claim. Pass 2 verified target frontmatter/tree/prompt drift and feedback-contract lints, rubric score 100, current SHA, and all four criteria. This static PASS does not claim that a Gate D live trial has already run.
